Remarks 20 
                         
                            
                               
                                  (1) 
                                  Since metric spaces are first-countable, the lemma above also holds true for metric spaces. 
                               
                               
                                  (2) 
                                  In the present paper, we will not assume that the topological spaces are first-countable. That is the reason why we shall use nets instead of sequences.

(1) 
                                  Since metric spaces are first-countable, the lemma above also holds true for metric spaces. 
                               
                               
                                  (2) 
                                  In the present paper, we will not assume that the topological spaces are first-countable. That is the reason why we shall use nets instead of sequences.

Since metric spaces are first-countable, the lemma above also holds true for metric spaces.

In the present paper, we will not assume that the topological spaces are first-countable. That is the reason why we shall use nets instead of sequences.

Since we will work with biorders defined between two (not necessarily disjoint) sets  
                         A 
                       and  
                         X 
                      , there are some differences with respect to the problem studied in Refs.  Bosi et al. (2015)  and  Estevan et al. (2013a) . First, the biorder generalizes the more particular case of an interval order. Notice too that the upper and lower contour sets of a biorder lie in distinct sets and not in the same one as in the case of interval orders. Besides, instead of working with a pair of functions  
                         
                            ( 
                            u 
                            , 
                            v 
                            ) 
                         
                       defined on a single topological space  
                         
                            ( 
                            X 
                            , 
                            τ 
                            ) 
                         
                      , now we deal with a pair of functions such that each of one is defined on its corresponding topological space (i.e.   
                         
                            ( 
                            A 
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  τ 
                               
                               
                                  A 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       or  
                         
                            ( 
                            X 
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  τ 
                               
                               
                                  X 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                      ). Therefore, we may be interested in the continuity of only one of the functions.

Despite these differences with the problems studied in  Bosi et al. (2015)  and  Estevan et al. (2013a) , the technique and the work-style are similar so, we will omit some proofs. Anyway, since these results on continuous representability of biorders are new and we will need them for Sections   4 and 5 , we collect them here in a brief manner.

Given a biorder  
                         ≺ 
                       from  
                         
                            ( 
                            A 
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  τ 
                               
                               
                                  A 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       to  
                         
                            ( 
                            X 
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  τ 
                               
                               
                                  X 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                      , we shall search for necessary conditions for the continuous representability of  
                         ≺ 
                      . We introduce here these necessary conditions in a brief manner.

Lemma 21 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  A 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        τ 
                                     
                                     
                                        A 
                                     
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            and  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  X 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        τ 
                                     
                                     
                                        X 
                                     
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            be two topological spaces. Let  
                            
                               ≺ 
                             
                            be a biorder from  
                            
                               A 
                             
                            to  
                            
                               X 
                            
                            . If  
                            
                               ≺ 
                             
                            is continuously representable on  
                            
                               A 
                            
                            , then the following properties hold true: 
                            
                               
                                  (N1) 
                                  
                                     The biorder has a countable strictly dense subset. 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  (N2) 
                                  
                                     The biorder is  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              τ 
                                           
                                           
                                              A 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     -continuous. 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  (N3) 
                                  
                                     If a net  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 ( 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       a 
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       j 
                                                    
                                                 
                                                 ) 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              j 
                                              ∈ 
                                              J 
                                           
                                        
                                        ⊆ 
                                        A 
                                      
                                     converges to two points  
                                     
                                        a 
                                        , 
                                        b 
                                        ∈ 
                                        A 
                                     
                                     , then  
                                     
                                        a 
                                        
                                           
                                              ∼ 
                                           
                                           
                                              ∗ 
                                           
                                        
                                        b 
                                     
                                     . 
                                  
                               
                               
                                  (N4) 
                                  
                                     If  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 ( 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       a 
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       j 
                                                    
                                                 
                                                 ) 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              j 
                                              ∈ 
                                              J 
                                           
                                        
                                        ⊆ 
                                        A 
                                      
                                     converges to  
                                     
                                        a 
                                        ∈ 
                                        A 
                                     
                                     , and  
                                     
                                        x 
                                        , 
                                        y 
                                        ∈ 
                                        X 
                                      
                                     are such that  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              a 
                                           
                                           
                                              j 
                                           
                                        
                                        ≺ 
                                        x 
                                        ≾ 
                                        a 
                                      
                                     and also  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              a 
                                           
                                           
                                              j 
                                           
                                        
                                        ≺ 
                                        y 
                                        ≾ 
                                        a 
                                      
                                     for every  
                                     
                                        j 
                                        ∈ 
                                        J 
                                     
                                     , then  
                                     
                                        x 
                                        
                                           
                                              ∼ 
                                           
                                           
                                              ∗ 
                                              ∗ 
                                           
                                        
                                        y 
                                     
                                     . 
                                  
                               
                            
                            Dually, if  
                            
                               ≺ 
                             
                            is continuously representable on  
                            
                               X

                                              N 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 1 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           )

                                           ( 
                                           
                                              N 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 2 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     
                                  
                                  
                                     The biorder is  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              τ 
                                           
                                           
                                              X 
                                           
                                        
                                     
                                     -continuous.

                                        , 
                                        y 
                                        ∈

                                              ∼ 
                                           
                                           
                                              ∗ 
                                              ∗

                                                 ′

                                              ∈ 
                                              J

                                        X

                                        X

                                        c 
                                        ∈ 
                                        A

                                        ≾ 
                                        b 
                                        ≺

                                     .

Proof 
                         We omit the proofs since similar results were already demonstrated in  Bosi et al. (2015)  for interval orders (see also  Estevan et al., 2013a ). □

We omit the proofs since similar results were already demonstrated in  Bosi et al. (2015)  for interval orders (see also  Estevan et al., 2013a ). □

                                  (2)

                                              ∗ 
                                           
                                        
                                     ) is  
                                        
                                           
                                              τ 
                                           
                                           
                                              A 
                                           
                                        
                                     -continuous (resp.  
                                        
                                           
                                              τ 
                                           
                                           
                                              X 
                                           
                                        
                                     -continuous), then condition (N3) and (N4) (resp. ( 
                                        
                                           N 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              3 
                                           
                                           
                                              ′ 
                                           
                                        
                                     ) and ( 
                                        
                                           N 
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              4 
                                           
                                           
                                              ′ 
                                           
                                        
                                     )) are trivially satisfied. The converse implication is not true, as we have seen with the continuously representable biorder of  Example 11 , whose trace  
                                        
                                           
                                              ≾ 
                                           
                                           
                                              ∗ 
                                           
                                        
                                      fails to be  
                                        
                                           
                                              τ 
                                           
                                           
                                              A 
                                           
                                        
                                     -continuous.

Let us prove now that these necessary condition (N1)–(N4) are  independent  (analogously for the dual necessary conditions ( 
                         
                            N 
                         
                         
                            
                               1 
                            
                            
                               ′ 
                            
                         
                      )–( 
                         
                            N 
                         
                         
                            
                               4 
                            
                            
                               ′ 
                            
                         
                      )), so that none of these conditions is sufficient by itself, in the general case. To this purpose, we include four examples in order to conclude that each one of the condition (N1), (N2), (N3) and (N4) does not depend on the remaining ones.

                             so, it fails to satisfy (N3).

Remark 27 
                         In the present section, we studied some necessary (and independent) conditions for the continuous representability of a biorder. However, there is no result on the sufficiency of these conditions: the problem is open, at least for the general case. Nevertheless, in next Section   4 , we achieve a characterization of the continuous representability of a biorder when each function also represents a total preorder that refines the corresponding trace, and in Section   5  we prove that those necessary conditions are sufficient at least when the quotient sets  
                               A 
                               / 
                               
                                  
                                     ∼ 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗ 
                                  
                               
                             and  
                               X 
                               / 
                               
                                  
                                     ∼ 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗ 
                                     ∗ 
                                  
                               
                             are finite.

In the present section, we studied some necessary (and independent) conditions for the continuous representability of a biorder. However, there is no result on the sufficiency of these conditions: the problem is open, at least for the general case. Nevertheless, in next Section   4 , we achieve a characterization of the continuous representability of a biorder when each function also represents a total preorder that refines the corresponding trace, and in Section   5  we prove that those necessary conditions are sufficient at least when the quotient sets  
                               A 
                               / 
                               
                                  
                                     ∼ 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗ 
                                  
                               
                             and  
                               X 
                               / 
                               
                                  
                                     ∼ 
                                  
                                  
                                     ∗ 
                                     ∗ 
                                  
                               
                             are finite.

There are distinct reasons that motivate the study of a biorder defined from a totally preordered set to another one, the context (see  Example 11 ) may be one of them. But there is another practical reason that justifies the study of this section. As  Example 11  shows (see also  Remark 12 ), there are biorders that can only be continuously represented just if we renounce to represent the traces. Therefore, instead of working with traces, we can work with other total preorders that  refine  the traces in order to obtain continuous representations. (Here, when we say that a total preorder  
                         
                            
                               ≾ 
                            
                            
                               r 
                            
                         
                       on  
                         X 
                       
                      refines  a trace  
                         ≾ 
                      , it means that the condition  
                         
                            { 
                            x 
                            ≺ 
                            y 
                            ⟹ 
                            x 
                            
                               
                                  ≺ 
                               
                               
                                  r 
                               
                            
                            y 
                            } 
                         
                       is satisfied, for any  
                         x 
                         , 
                         y 
                         ∈ 
                         X 
                      .)

Moreover, as we already deeply explained in the Introduction and motivation, there are other two important and remarkable reasons. On one hand, from an applied point of view this kind of structures studied in the present section represent and generalize the mathematical idea of a distributed system ( Lamport, 1978 ) (in the field of computation). Furthermore, these structures are quite common too in causality ( Panangaden, 2014 ), related to the relativity theory (see also  Busemann, 1967 ;  Kronheimer & Penrose, 1966 ). On the other hand, since some preorders can be interpreted as a biorder (or as a family of biorders) between totally preordered sets, the main result of this section could be a key in order to achieve a characterization of the continuous finite Richter–Peleg multi-utility of some kind of preorders ( Alcantud et al., 2016 ;  Peleg, 1970; Richter, 1966 ).

The present section is directly related to the aforementioned two main motivations: distributed systems and Richter–Peleg multi-utilities of preorders. That is the reason why here  
                         A 
                       and  
                         X 
                       will denote two disjoint sets, so any biorder from  
                         A 
                       to  
                         X 
                       is asymmetric on  
                         A 
                         ∪ 
                         X 
                      . For more clarity, in this section, we shall denote a biorder relation by  
                         P 
                       in order to better distinguish it from other relations (mainly preorders and total preorders, which are denoted as usual by  
                         ≾ 
                      ). The corresponding traces of the biorder shall be denoted again by  
                         
                            
                               ≾ 
                            
                            
                               ∗ 
                            
                         
                       and  
                         
                            
                               ≾ 
                            
                            
                               ∗ 
                               ∗ 
                            
                         
                      . We shall denote by  
                         x 
                         R 
                         a 
                       when  
                         ¬ 
                         
                            ( 
                            a 
                            P 
                            x 
                            ) 
                         
                      , for any  
                         a 
                         ∈ 
                         A 
                       and  
                         x 
                         ∈ 
                         X 
                      .

In the following pages, we mathematically formalize the concept of a distributed system with two processes (the original and ‘non-mathematical’ definitions of these concepts–an event, a process and a distributed system–have been introduced in Section   1 ). Then, we study–from a mathematical point of view–the representability of this case in which the distributed system is made by two processes (totally preordered sets) and a communication between them in both directions (two biorders). But first, before this mathematical definition of a distributed system, notice that given  
                         
                            ( 
                            A 
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  ≾ 
                               
                               
                                  A 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       and  
                         
                            ( 
                            X 
                            , 
                            
                               
                                  ≾ 
                               
                               
                                  X 
                               
                            
                            ) 
                         
                       two totally preordered sets, it is possible to construct distinct biorders between them such that the corresponding causal precedences coincide. In order to see that, the reader can compare the biordered pair of  Fig. 3 
                       with  Fig. 1  and check that the corresponding causal precedences are actually the same.

The explanation of this coincidence is that, since the relation  
                         c 
                         P 
                         x 
                       (in  Fig. 3 ) does not include new information with respect to the causal precedence,  Fig. 1  is actually a transitive reduction (see  Definition 1 ) of  Fig. 3 . Therefore, the paths of causality are the same in both cases. This lack of uniqueness motivates us to work with the transitive closure (see  Definition 1 ) of the order structure and, hence, with biorders that satisfy some ‘good’ properties.

Since

Anyway, from an applied point of view, since in the theory of distributed systems there always exist a sender of the message and a receiver, we can work just with the transitive reduction. That is, from an applied point of view, since given  
                         a 
                         P 
                         x 
                       there always exist the maximum of  
                         
                            
                               L

                            P 
                            x

                       (the receiver), then we can work just with the transitive reduction. However, from a theoretical point of view, we will consider all the possible paths (that is, the transitive closure).

The aforementioned four implications can be characterized as follows:

Now, we are ready to propose a definition of a  distributed system of two sets  from a mathematical and theoretical point of view (the original definitions of these concepts–an event, a process and a distributed system–have been introduced in Section   1 ):  
                         Definition 29 
                         Let  
                               
                                  ( 
                                  A 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        ≾ 
                                     
                                     
                                        A 
                                     
                                  
                                  )

                                  ( 
                                  X 
                                  ,

                                        X 
                                     
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             be two totally preordered sets. Each totally preordered set (

                                  A 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        ≾

                                        ≾ 
                                     
                                     
                                        X

                            ) is said to be a  process . Each element of the processes (

                                  A 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        ≾ 
                                     
                                     
                                        A

                             and

                                  X

                                        X

                               P

                               A 
                             to  
                               X

                               X

                               A 
                            , respectively. Then, we say that this biordered structure

                                  ×

Remarks 30 
                         
                            
                               
                                  (i) 
                                  It is easy to check that the corresponding causal precedence of the biordered structure of the definition above is actually a strict preorder. 
                               
                               
                                  (ii) 
                                  Notice that one (or both) biorder may be empty, so that there is no communication in one direction (or both). In this case, we shall denote  
                                        P 
                                        = 
                                        0̸ 
                                     .

(i) 
                                  It is easy to check that the corresponding causal precedence of the biordered structure of the definition above is actually a strict preorder. 
                               
                               
                                  (ii) 
                                  Notice that one (or both) biorder may be empty, so that there is no communication in one direction (or both). In this case, we shall denote  
                                        P 
                                        = 
                                        0̸ 
                                     .

It is easy to check that the corresponding causal precedence of the biordered structure of the definition above is actually a strict preorder.

Notice that one (or both) biorder may be empty, so that there is no communication in one direction (or both). In this case, we shall denote  
                                        P 
                                        = 
                                        0̸ 
                                     .

                            . Therefore, since the preorders refine the traces, it holds that  
                               a

                            . We reason dually for the biorders from  
                               X 
                             to  
                               A

                            : This implication is trivial. □

If the sets  
                               A

                               X 
                             are endowed with a topology, then we will say that the distributed system is  continuously representable  if there exists a continuous representation.

Now, we are ready for the study of a distributed system of two sets. We shall focus our attention on the continuous representability of this structure as well as on the representability of the corresponding causal precedence.

If we want to represent the biorder  
                         P 
                       through a pair of functions  
                         
                            ( 
                            u 
                            , 
                            v 
                            ) 
                         
                       that also represents the total preorders  
                         
                            
                               ≾

                               ≾ 
                            
                            
                               X

                         u 
                       and  
                         v

                            Let

                         (We only prove part (a) of property (

                                           a 
                                           , 
                                           b

                                              u 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           A 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     . In other words, Case 3 is impossible.

Once the extension

                                  )

                               =

                               x 
                               ∈

                                              ∈ 
                                              Λ 
                                           
                                        
                                      is quasi-constant. Indeed, for every index  
                                        β 
                                      with  
                                        
                                           
                                              α 
                                           
                                           
                                              0 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              ≺ 
                                           
                                           
                                              Λ 
                                           
                                        
                                        β 
                                     , it holds that  
                                        V 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           
                                              
                                                 x 
                                              
                                              
                                                 β 
                                              
                                           
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        = 
                                        V 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           x 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     . Obviously, this implies that the net  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 ( 
                                                 V 
                                                 
                                                    ( 
                                                    
                                                       
                                                          x 
                                                       
                                                       
                                                          α 
                                                       
                                                    
                                                    ) 
                                                 
                                                 ) 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              α 
                                              ∈ 
                                              Λ 
                                           
                                        
                                      converges to  
                                        V 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           x 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     , so that  
                                        V 
                                      is continuous at  
                                        x 
                                     .

Case 2: It holds that  
                                        
                                           
                                              v 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           x 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        = 
                                        b 
                                     , and  
                                        b 
                                      is not an isolated point of  
                                        
                                           
                                              v 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           X 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      with respect to the restriction to  
                                        
                                           
                                              v 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           X 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      of the usual Euclidean topology on  
                                        R 
                                     , so that for every  
                                        ϵ 
                                        > 
                                        0 
                                      we have that  
                                        
                                           
                                              v 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           X 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        ⋂ 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           b 
                                           − 
                                           ϵ 
                                           , 
                                           b 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        ≠ 
                                        0̸ 
                                     . Since  
                                        
                                           
                                              v 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                      is already continuous, it is enough to check the continuity of  
                                        
                                           
                                              H 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                      at  
                                        b 
                                        = 
                                        
                                           
                                              v 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           x 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     . Since we are now working directly on the real line  
                                        R 
                                     , there is no loss of generality in considering a strictly increasing sequence  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 ( 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       y 
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       n 
                                                    
                                                 
                                                 ) 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              n 
                                              ∈ 
                                              N 
                                           
                                        
                                      such that  
                                        
                                           
                                              H 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           
                                              
                                                 y 
                                              
                                              
                                                 n 
                                              
                                           
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      is defined for every  
                                        n 
                                        ∈ 
                                        N 
                                      and the limit of  
                                        
                                           
                                              y 
                                           
                                           
                                              n 
                                           
                                        
                                      is  
                                        b 
                                     . Since  
                                        
                                           
                                              v 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           X 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        ⋂ 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           b 
                                           − 
                                           ϵ 
                                           , 
                                           b 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        ≠ 
                                        0̸ 
                                      for every  
                                        ϵ 
                                        > 
                                        0 
                                     , given  
                                        
                                           
                                              y 
                                           
                                           
                                              n 
                                           
                                        
                                      we may find an element  
                                        
                                           
                                              z 
                                           
                                           
                                              n 
                                           
                                        
                                        ∈ 
                                        R 
                                        ∖ 
                                        
                                           
                                              B 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    u 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    ̄ 
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                      such that  
                                        
                                           
                                              y 
                                           
                                           
                                              n 
                                           
                                        
                                        < 
                                        
                                           
                                              z 
                                           
                                           
                                              n 
                                           
                                        
                                        < 
                                        b 
                                      Also, given  
                                        
                                           
                                              z 
                                           
                                           
                                              n 
                                           
                                        
                                     , there exists  
                                        k 
                                        ∈ 
                                        N 
                                      such that  
                                        
                                           
                                              z 
                                           
                                           
                                              n 
                                           
                                        
                                        < 
                                        
                                           
                                              y 
                                           
                                           
                                              n 
                                              + 
                                              k 
                                           
                                        
                                        < 
                                        b 
                                     . Thus, the sequence  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 ( 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       z 
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       n 
                                                    
                                                 
                                                 ) 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              n 
                                              ∈ 
                                              N 
                                           
                                        
                                      also converges to  
                                        b 
                                     . Notice now that  
                                        H 
                                      is continuous and strictly increasing in  
                                        R 
                                        ∖ 
                                        
                                           
                                              B 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    u 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    ̄ 
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                     , and for any strictly increasing sequence  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 ( 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       z 
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       n 
                                                    
                                                 
                                                 ) 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              n 
                                              ∈ 
                                              N 
                                           
                                        
                                      in  
                                        R 
                                        ∖ 
                                        
                                           
                                              B 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 
                                                    u 
                                                 
                                                 
                                                    ̄ 
                                                 
                                              
                                           
                                        
                                      that converges to  
                                        b 
                                      it holds that  
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 ( 
                                                 H 
                                                 
                                                    ( 
                                                    
                                                       
                                                          z 
                                                       
                                                       
                                                          n 
                                                       
                                                    
                                                    ) 
                                                 
                                                 ) 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              n 
                                              ∈ 
                                              N 
                                           
                                        
                                      converges to  
                                        H 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           a 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        = 
                                        
                                           
                                              H 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           b 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        = 
                                        
                                           
                                              H 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           
                                              
                                                 v 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ̄ 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              x 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     . Hence, the sequence  
                                        
                                           
                                              H 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           
                                              
                                                 ( 
                                                 
                                                    
                                                       y 
                                                    
                                                    
                                                       n 
                                                    
                                                 
                                                 ) 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              n 
                                              ∈ 
                                              N 
                                           
                                        
                                      also converges to  
                                        H 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           a 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        = 
                                        
                                           
                                              H 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           b 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        = 
                                        
                                           
                                              H 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           
                                              
                                                 v 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ̄ 
                                              
                                           
                                           
                                              ( 
                                              x 
                                              ) 
                                           
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     , so that  
                                        
                                           
                                              H 
                                           
                                           
                                              ̄ 
                                           
                                        
                                      is indeed continuous at  
                                        b 
                                     .

This concludes the proof. □

Remarks 36 
                         
                            
                               
                                  (1) 
                                  In  Theorem 35 , since we obtain a continuous representation of a biorder  
                                        P 
                                     , it must satisfy the necessary conditions (N1)–(N4) and

                                           ( 
                                           
                                              N 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 1 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ) 
                                        
                                        – 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           
                                              N 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 4 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ) 
                                        
                                     . In fact, (N1) and (N2)– 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           
                                              N 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 2 
                                              
                                              
                                                 ′ 
                                              
                                           
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      are trivially satisfied since we are working with a bicontinuous and representable biorder, (N3)– 
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           
                                              N 
                                           
                                           
                                              
                                                 3

                                  (2) 
                                  A similar proof (but for a distinct result) in which two functions are modified in order to arrive at a pair of continuous functions is done in Theorem 5 of  Bosi et al. (2015) . There, two functions  
                                        
                                           ( 
                                           u 
                                           , 
                                           v 
                                           ) 
                                        
                                      that represent a semiorder as well as the main trace are modified in order to achieve a pair of continuous ones. Here, in  Theorem 35  of the present paper, we reason analogously, but now working on two sets (instead of on a single one) and with two functions such that each of them also represents a total preorder previously defined on each set (so, now the functions do not represent the same total preorder). An abstraction of this kind of proofs (in which a family of functions are modified in order to become them continuous, but always keeping some conditions between them) is made in the paper entitled  Generalized Debreu’s Open Gap Lemma  ( Estevan, 2016 ).

The next corollary is deduced from  Theorem 35  and  Problem 22  (4).  
                         Corollary 37
