Remark 38 
                         Notice that for the proof of  Theorem 35 , it is not necessary that the sets  
                               A 
                             and  
                               X 
                             are disjoint (this fact was assumed in the present section just for the context of the two main motivations). Therefore, if the biorder is actually an interval order on a single set  
                               X 
                            , then the continuity of the traces and the bicontinuity of the biorder is equivalent to the concept of  natural topology  introduced in  Bosi, Campión et al. (2007) . So, in this case, the  Corollary 37  recovers the main result Theorem 1 obtained in  Bosi, Campión et al. (2007)  (see also  Chateauneuf, 1987 ).

Notice that for the proof of  Theorem 35 , it is not necessary that the sets  
                               A 
                             and  
                               X 
                             are disjoint (this fact was assumed in the present section just for the context of the two main motivations). Therefore, if the biorder is actually an interval order on a single set  
                               X 
                            , then the continuity of the traces and the bicontinuity of the biorder is equivalent to the concept of  natural topology  introduced in  Bosi, Campión et al. (2007) . So, in this case, the  Corollary 37  recovers the main result Theorem 1 obtained in  Bosi, Campión et al. (2007)  (see also  Chateauneuf, 1987 ).

Next  Corollary 39  characterizes the continuous representability of a distributed system of two processes and it is deduced from  Theorem 35 .  
                         Corollary 39 
                         
                            Let  
                            
                               
                                  ( 
                                  A 
                                  × 
                                  X 
                                  , 
                                  
                                     
                                        ≾ 
                                     
                                     
                                        A 
                                     
                                  
                                  × 
                                  
                                     
                                        ≾ 
                                     
                                     
                                        X 
                                     
                                  
                                  , 
                                  P 
                                  × 
                                  
                                     
                                        P 
                                     
                                     
                                        ′ 
                                     
                                  
                                  ) 
                               
                             
                            be a representable distributed system. Then, it is continuous representable if and only if the statements of     
                               Theorem   35     hold true for each biorder and each total preorder. That is, if and only if the following statements hold true: 
                            
                               
                                  (a) 
                                  
                                     The total preorders  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              ≾ 
                                           
                                           
                                              A 
                                           
                                        
                                      
                                     and  
                                     
                                        
                                           
                                              ≾ 
                                           
                                           
                                              X 
                                           
                                        
                                      
                                     are

Corollary 47

Remark 48 
                         As we have said in  Remark 42 , the converse implication of this corollary is not true in general.

As we have said in  Remark 42 , the converse implication of this corollary is not true in general.

Since there was a certain lack on continuous representability of biorders, as well as on biorders defined on total preordered spaces, this paper provides some new results to the literature.

Furthermore, in Section   4 , we study the well motivated case of biorders defined between totally preordered spaces. Besides achieving a characterization of the continuous representability, it is also remarkable the usefulness of these structures in computer sciences and causality. Thus, we have also formalized a mathematical definition of these order structures, also named by distributed system with two processes in computing. By the way, we use this new knowledge in order to purpose a new technique for the (continuous) representability of some preorders, just those which are a causal precedence (also named ‘happened before’ relation) of a distributed system. In order to formalize completely the concept of distributed system, a further study must be done, but now linking biorders between  
                         n 
                       totally preordered sets. The author expects to continue this line providing some mathematical tools to study these structures in computing or physics.

Moreover, since  Theorem 35  deals with biorders and total preorders (instead of the more particular case of traces), it also supposes an improvement on the topic of interval orders. Finally, notice that the problem of characterizing the continuous representability of biorders remains still open. However, necessary conditions have been introduced in Section   3 , and proved to be sufficient (in Section   5 ) when the biorder is such that the quotient sets  
                         A 
                         / 
                         
                            
                               ∼ 
                            
                            
                               ∗ 
                            
                         
                       and  
                         X 
                         / 
                         
                            
                               ∼ 
                            
                            
                               ∗ 
                               ∗ 
                            
                         
                       are finite (and also for the particular case in which  
                         A 
                       and  
                         X 
                       are finite).
